channel.title: Posts on Andrey Perestoronin's Blog
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on Andrey Perestoronin's Blog

item.title: Results Of 2025
item.description:
These are my 2025 year‑end results:

  • I graduated from university with a Bachelor’s degree in Applied Computer Science and defended my diploma on the topic “Development of Business Architecture for an Enterprise in the Field of Continuing Professional Education”. I also obtained a diploma of professional retraining in IT project management.

  • I tried several jobs, working as a sales representative for one of the largest Russian banks’ investment products, known as Tinkoff Bank. In the summer, I tried P2P crypto trading on the Bybit and HTX exchanges. In the fourth quarter of the year, I worked as a salesperson for rolled metal products and educational courses. I didn’t find my calling in any of these roles, but I gained a lot of experience. I made little money, and my credit card debt remained the same - but I didn’t use the card at all in 2025, which is also a positive outcome.

item.title: How I Learn English
item.description:
Thanks to the progress I’ve made in learning English, I’d like to share some useful resources and talk about my learning plan.

Russian is my native language, which I speak fluently. I started learning English when I was 15 - almost 8 years ago. I remember learning it by watching The Simpsons and noting down and repeating new vocabulary. It was a rather inconvenient approach. After graduating from school at 18 and going to university, I stopped practising English, although I had reached a B2 level.

item.title: The Power of Honey
item.description:
Honey is more than just regular white table sugar - it contains many unique beneficial components:

  1. Honey doesn’t splash when you hit the surface with a spoon. This is a way to check if it’s adulterated

  2. Look for honey from local beekeepers. This makes it easier to avoid fake or adulterated honey

  3. Scoop up honey with a wooden spoon. Avoid contact with metal

  4. Avoid mixing honey with liquids heated above 40-50℃

item.title: What I Cant Live Without
item.description:
There are 10 things I can’t live (I can) without below.

  1. Thermos for 1 L. I am fond of tea, so I cannot image a day without it. Having a hot Puer or Ivan-tea nearby, anywhere, in winter or summer, is a pleasure

  2. Knitted socks. Feet need to be warm and if they shiver from the cold at night, it’s impossible to sleep. Warm socks help a lot in this situation

item.title: My dear cat
item.description: This is a page about »My Dear Cat«. Winter, 2024-2025
